mainEntity.name: Can I get benefits if my work injury just made an old problem worse?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. If a work accident aggravates a pre-existing condition so you can no longer work, it is a compensable claim. Insurance companies often deny these, but Utah law protects workers if the accident was the 'contributing cause' of the disability.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If your work injury aggravated a pre-existing condition (like an old back injury or degenerative disc disease) to the point where you can no longer work, you are still eligible for benefits in Utah. The law states that as long as the workplace accident was the 'legal cause' and 'medical cause' of the current disability, the insurance company is liable. They often try to deny these claims by blaming your age or prior health.

The legal standard is whether the work event contributed significantly to your current inability to labor.

- 'Apportionment' may reduce the payout if part of the disability is purely pre-existing.
- Medical records showing you were working fine before the accident are crucial.
- Expert medical testimony is often needed to link the 'flare-up' to the work event.

Never tell an adjuster 'my back has always hurt' without context; clarify that the work injury made it impossible to continue. If a denial is based on a pre-existing condition, an appeal is necessary to prove the 'aggravation' rule applies.
